item.text: The remaining issue cannot be solved reliably from the text alone: user draft lines can also start with `chat> `. I’m adding a failing production-path test for that sentinel case first, then I’ll pass explicit prompt-start metadata from the app/harness into `TerminalUi` so it can distinguish the real prompt marker from user-entered text.

Run required checks and real-agent verification

The sentinel regression is in place. I’m running it alone now to confirm it fails on the current `rposition("chat> ")` logic.

item.text: The test fails as expected: the fake prompt marker inside the draft shifts the truncation point. I’m adding explicit prompt-start-line metadata through the app/harness render and key paths now.

items.text: Add failing regression for draft line starting with chat prompt marker
Pass prompt-start metadata through terminal app and harness contexts
Use prompt metadata for visible-content splitting and yank filtering
Run required checks and real-agent verification

item.text: First I’m adding a cached `right_prompt_start_line` to `TerminalUi` and extending the crate-internal context methods. Public render methods will keep their current signatures and fall back to heuristic parsing for direct tests.
Now I’m threading the prompt-start line into key handling. This is crate-internal, so it won’t affect the public `TerminalUi::handle_key` API.
I’m adding the visible-content metadata struct next, then I’ll update rendering so the cache remains correct while a visual selection is displayed.
The render path is being split into a shared internal method so `TerminalApp` and `UiHarness` can render with exact prompt metadata, while existing public render calls still work.
Rendering now preserves prompt metadata in the cache. Next I’m changing `visible_right_content` into a metadata-returning helper and updating selection/yank filtering to use the cached prompt line.
I found the remaining cache helper. I’m adding the prompt-start parameter there and then switching the right-pane line helpers to use the cached exact line.

item.text: The cache now stores the visible prompt-start line. I’m switching `focus_cursor_text_position` and `pane_line_texts` to use that exact cached line instead of scanning for `chat> ` text.
Now I’m changing the selectable-line helper itself: with metadata it truncates at the exact prompt line; without metadata it keeps the old heuristic fallback for direct `TerminalUi` callers.

The filtering helper is metadata-aware. I’m updating `visible_content` so it computes the visible prompt-start line after scroll/tail trimming, and adding a line-index splitter for the exact prompt line.
I need the lower helper section, so I’m opening the `visible_content` area and patching it directly.
The UI helper now returns both visible text and the visible prompt-start line. I’m updating `UiHarness` to provide prompt metadata in both render and key paths.
That patch duplicated `render_frame` instead of replacing it cleanly. I’m correcting that immediately.
I’m replacing the duplicated block with the intended single render method.
I’m applying a focused patch to keep only the context-aware `render_frame`.
The duplicate is fixed. I’m adding `right_content_context` in the harness and updating key handling to pass the prompt-start line.

The harness now passes exact prompt metadata. I’m doing the same in `TerminalAppCore`, where the prompt-start line is derived from the transcript lines before `terminal_right_content` appends `chat> `.

`TerminalAppCore` now computes and passes the prompt line. I’m adding a small transcript line counter so embedded newlines in transcript entries are counted the same way `terminal_right_content` renders them.

items.text: Add failing regression for draft line starting with chat prompt marker
Pass prompt-start metadata through terminal app and harness contexts
Use prompt metadata for visible-content splitting and yank filtering
Run required checks and real-agent verification

item.text:
Fixed the sentinel case where a user-entered draft line starts with `chat> `.

The fix no longer guesses the prompt from the last `chat> ` text line. `TerminalAppCore` and `UiHarness` now pass the real prompt-start line into `TerminalUi`, and `TerminalUi` carries that through visible-content caching and right-pane yank filtering. See [src/terminal_app.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/src/terminal_app.rs:483), [src/ui_harness.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/src/ui_harness.rs:120), and [src/ui.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/src/ui.rs:399).

`selectable_right_text_lines` now truncates at the cached prompt line when available, and `visible_content` preserves the visible prompt line after scrolling/trimming. See [src/ui.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/src/ui.rs:1714) and [src/ui.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/src/ui.rs:1877).

Added a failing-first regression for `first draft\nchat> quoted draft` at [tests/terminal_app.rs](/home/user/src/work-leaf/.bench-tmp/work-leaf-3feature-worktree-bench.if2eWj/worktrees/feature-1/tests/terminal_app.rs:439).

Validation:
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets --all-features`
- Real Codex smoke succeeded: `thread.started`, then `WORK_LEAF_REAL_AGENT_OK`.

Changes are uncommitted.
